steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $\frac{7x-4}{3}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Factor the numerator
steps.content: Both terms in the numerator share the factor $(x-6)$: $$7x(x-6)-4(x-6)=(x-6)(7x-4).$$

steps.title: Factor the denominator and cancel
steps.content: Factor $3$ from the denominator: $3x-18=3(x-6)$. Because $x\gt 6$, the factor $(x-6)$ is nonzero and can be canceled: $$\frac{(x-6)(7x-4)}{3(x-6)}=\frac{7x-4}{3}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $\frac{x-6}{3}$ — keeps the canceled factor instead of the remaining $7x-4$ factor. Eliminated.
  • B) $\frac{7x-4}{3}$ — matches the simplified expression.
  • C) $\frac{7x^{2}-4x-18}{3x-18}$ — equals $\frac{x(7x-4)-18}{3(x-6)}$, which does not equal the original. Eliminated.
  • D) $\frac{7x^{2}-4x-66}{3x-18}$ — $7x^{2}-4x-66$ is not divisible by $(x-6)$: plugging in $x=6$ gives $7(36)-4(6)-66=252-24-66=162\neq 0$, so it cannot simplify to $\frac{7x-4}{3}$. Eliminated.
B is correct.
